title: AnalyticsIdentity
description: Identity resolution table. Maps anonymous_id (uuid, browser/device generated on first visit) → identified_user_id (usr_...) as soon as the user identifies. `identify()` verb writes an identity row; `alias()` verb merges two identities that turned out to be the same person. Downstream MergeAliasedEventsJob rewrites subject_id on prior events from the anonymous_id to the identified user_id, so pre-signup behavior funnels into the post-signup profile.

anonymous_id.description: UUID generated browser-side on first visit. Unique per tenant — the frontend `@stackra/analytics` package writes it to a first-party cookie AND local storage for cross-session persistence.

identified_user_id.description: Populated when the caller invokes analytics.identify(user_id). Null before identify, becomes non-null AT identify time, may transition back to null on deidentification / GDPR erasure.

first_seen_at.description: When we first observed this anonymous_id — the timestamp of the very first analytics event carrying it.

identified_at.description: When identity_id became known. Retained even if identified_user_id later reverts to null (deidentification).

last_seen_at.description: Updated on every analytics event carrying this anonymous_id. Enables the anti-abuse job to purge stale identities with no activity for 90+ days.

device_fingerprint_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of a browser/device fingerprint. NEVER raw fingerprint. Used ONLY for anti-abuse (detect coordinated signup patterns from the same device). Never for cross-device tracking without explicit consent.

merged_from.description: Array of prior AnalyticsIdentity.id (aid_...) values that were merged INTO this row via alias(). Preserves the merge trail for support debugging.

indexes.name: analytics_identities_tenant_anonymous_uidx
indexes.description: Composite unique on (tenant, anonymous_id) among non-deleted rows. Primary lookup path when incoming events carry an anonymous_id.

indexes.name: analytics_identities_identified_user_idx
indexes.description: Reverse lookup: 'all anonymous identities belonging to user X' (useful for cross-device merge candidates).

indexes.name: analytics_identities_tenant_activity_idx
indexes.description: ReconcileAnalyticsIdentityJob scans by last_seen_at to purge stale anonymous identities.

indexes.name: analytics_identities_fingerprint_idx
indexes.description: Anti-abuse query: 'how many identities share this fingerprint hash?' — coordinated signup detector.

is_identified.description: Computed. True iff identified_user_id IS NOT NULL.

session_span_days.description: Computed. Days between first_seen_at and last_seen_at.

notes: device_fingerprint_hash HIDDEN on every wire — hashed value, but still an anti-abuse signal.
anonymous_id is visible to tenant admins for support debugging (correlating a browser session to events).
identified_user_id can be null-ed via POST /identities/{id}/deidentify (Enterprise + explicit user request) — audit row written.
